missing_info: Provide the exact interface state snapshots (output of "show interfaces Serial0/1/1" or equivalent) for R2 and R3 at the time the alert triggered and the current output; include admin/oper state, "line protocol" text, last-change timestamp, input/output/CRC errors, and last-clear time.

Provide syslog / event log lines from R2 and R3 spanning at least five minutes before and after the alert timestamp so we can see what caused the oper/admin state transitions (messages for line protocol, interface down/up, shutdown/no shutdown, or config changes).

Provide the running configuration for interface Serial0/1/1 on R2 and R3 ("show running-config interface Serial0/1/1") and any config-change/audit records (who/when committed) to confirm whether an admin shutdown or other config change was applied.

plan_history.intent_description: Serial0/1/1 on R2 moved from oper up to lower-layer-down while admin is up; Serial0/1/1 on R3 moved from oper up to down and admin set to down. Impact: point-to-point serial link between R2 and R3 is down causing loss of connectivity. Desired outcome: restore Serial0/1/1 on R2 and R3 to oper up with correct admin state so connectivity is resumed.
plan_history.problem: Point-to-point link Serial0/1/1 between router2 and router3 is down: router2 oper=lower-layer-down admin=up; router3 oper=down admin=down. Connectivity lost.
plan_history.fix_summary: Bring interface admin state to up on both endpoints to reinitialize the serial link: (1) issue no shutdown on router2 Serial0/1/1 (restarts line protocol negotiation) and (2) issue no shutdown on router3 Serial0/1/1 (undo admin down). Operational risk: temporary traffic interruption and possible interface flap during re-establishment.
